page: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. It is configured with 4 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core, and 200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The CPU configuration includes 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, backed by 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance in the top 25 percent. However, multi-threaded scaling is more moderate; stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average tier, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak, landing in the bottom 25 percent. Memory performance is robust, with write bandwidth for large blocks and the PassMark memory score both ranking in the top 25 percent, though read bandwidth remains average.

From a workload perspective, the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance as a memory-optimized resource suitable for memory-intensive applications that do not require dedicated CPU cores. While it handles Redis SET operations with average throughput, its database operations and static web serving benchmarks are weak. Consequently, it is best suited for caching layers, memory-heavy general-purpose workloads, and lightweight text generation tasks rather than high-throughput database hosting or intensive multi-threaded parallel processing.
description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory write bandwidth, though multi-threaded performance and database operations are more limited. This hardware profile provides a cost-effective option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU threads. It is well-suited for caching systems, memory-heavy general-purpose applications, and moderate single-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-200s-amd is a memory-optimized instance with 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 200 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-heavy workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring shared AMD Genoa processors and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.

200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in benchmarks
Strong memory write bandwidth and single-threaded compression
Best suited for caching layers and memory-intensive general-purpose tasks
